steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says archaeological evidence of "significant increases in clam size and abundance" concurrent with the Kwakwaka'wakw terraced garden practice "supports the conclusion that ______". The correct conclusion must be directly supported by that specific evidence — larger, more abundant clams — and nothing beyond it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the practice used by ancestors of modern Kwakwaka'wakw people not only effectively maintained a food source but also promoted its robustness.’: increased size and abundance directly show the clam population was maintained and grew stronger. This is exactly what the evidence supports. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘non-Indigenous people around the Pacific Northwest coast adopted the practice developed by the Kwakwaka'wakw people after observing its efficacy.’: the passage says nothing about non-Indigenous adoption of the practice. This goes beyond the evidence. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘there is greater corroboration in the archaeological record of ecological practices described in Kwakwaka'wakw songs than of those described in Temiar and Lakota songs.’: the passage never compares the archaeological support for different groups' songs. Only the Kwakwaka'wakw evidence is discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘although contemporary Kwakwaka'wakw people have a deep understanding of and appreciation for the fishing and farming practices used by their ancestors, they no longer implement those methods.’: the passage gives no information about whether the practice is still used today. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
